verifyNote: Immigration rules change frequently. Every value here MUST be re-verified on uscis.gov before relying on it, and confirmed with an immigration attorney for your situation.
todoNote: TODO (verify before launch on uscis.gov): (1) the Oct 30, 2025 removal of EAD auto-extension and its exact applicability to H-4 c(26); (2) current I-765 processing-time ranges for c(26) initial and renewal; (3) whether premium processing (I-907) is available for c(26) H-4 EAD; (4) the 180-day early-filing window.
filingWindowNote: USCIS generally lets you file an I-765 renewal up to 180 days before your current EAD expires. File as early in this window as possible.

autoExtension.summary: A DHS Interim Final Rule effective October 30, 2025 removed the automatic extension of EADs on timely-filed renewals.

preRule.appliesIf: I-765 renewal RECEIVED before 2025-10-30 (receipt date on Form I-797C)
preRule.capNote: Extension ran to the EARLIER of 540 days from the EAD 'Card Expires' date OR the end date of your H-4 I-94. It also required staying in valid H-4 status (unexpired I-94).

postRule.note: No automatic extension. When the EAD expires you must STOP working until the new EAD is approved and received.

processing.postedVsActualNote: USCIS often posts ~6 months, but in practice many H-4 EAD (c(26)) cases take 9–14 months, and you usually can't even submit a service request until the case passes the posted time. Treat the high end as realistic.

premiumProcessing.note: Premium processing (Form I-907, 30-business-day window for many I-765 categories) MAY be available for some EAD categories. Confirm current eligibility for H-4 c(26) on uscis.gov before relying on it.

concurrentFiling.note: You can file the H-4 extension (I-539) and H-4 EAD (I-765) together with the H-1B I-129. But USCIS is no longer required to adjudicate them together (the concurrent-adjudication requirement expired Jan 18, 2025), so the EAD can lag the H-1B approval by many months.

dependencyRisk: An H-4 EAD is valid only while the H-4 status is valid, which depends on the H-1B spouse maintaining H-1B status. If the H-1B lapses (job loss, status issue), the H-4 and the EAD — and any job or business run on it — are affected.
